sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Back pain evaluation and also has a past medical history of diabetes high blood pressure and high cholesterol
History of Present Illness
Gabriel is a 43 -year-old male today here for back pain evaluation and also has a past medical history of diabetes high blood pressure and high cholesterol so gabriel tell me what's going on with your back He was working in the yard and you know bent over to pick something up and i got this pain and you know across the lower part of my back and then it went down your left leg and you know it's been going on for about four days and just does n't seem to be getting any better He reports that his wife held a gun to his head make him go out there work in the yard and carry some stuff around it's not his not his first choice but but that day i i lost the i lost the argument He has had occasional back pain over the years and has tried ibuprofen which helped some He has swelling in his ankles that started about a month or two after starting norvasc He is on metformin 500 milligrams twice a day and his blood pressure is typically around 120 over 70 and he checks it maybe once a week or two or three times a month He has had some swelling in his ankles and he was wondering if the two might be related
Findings

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Neck exam has no JVd there is no bruits that I can hear your lung exam no rales no wheezing on your heart exam you do have a two over six systolic ejection murmur you had that in the past so I'm not too worried about that otherwise regular rate and rhythm on your heart exam on your belly exam is nice and soft on your back exam you do have some tenderness on the left paraspinal area right where I'm pressing right there your straight leg raise test is negative your reflexes are normal you have some just some tenderness in the lower back in the paraspinal area of your back when I palpate there otherwise your neurological exam is normal on your extremity exam you do have this one plus nonpitting edema of your lower extremities which is a little bit of swelling in your ankles no calf tenderness negative homans sign no signs of blood clot that's what that means
Assessment
Clinical presentation is most consistent with a muscular sprain
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I'm recommending we start you on some anti-inflammatory naproxen 500 500 milligrams twice a day and flexeril 10 milligrams twice a day as well I'm going to refer you to for physical therapy to help strengthen some of the muscles in your lower back I don't think you need an x-ray at this stage we'll start with physical therapy and the muscle relaxers and the pain medicines if it does n't get better then we can get an x-ray and I'm going to order some blood work today I'm going to go ahead and stop the norvasc and we'll put you on some hydrochlorothiazide 10 milligrams once a day and if that does n't get if the swelling does n't go away we'll do more testing for right now and hopefully this will go away once we stop this medication since it started around that time
